Outdoor Supply Advice New Sources of Energy Well, they aren’t new sources; they‘re the oldest: sun, wind, earth heat (geothermal). But OK, to us they’re new, and indeed they’re exciting. The prospect of truly self- contained habitable energy systems is romantic country. There you are with your

Well, they aren’t new sources; they‘re the oldest: sun, wind, earth heat (geothermal). But OK, to us they’re new, and indeed they’re exciting. The prospect of truly self- contained habitable energy systems is romantic country. There you are with your friends on your hill putting sun and wind through useful changes that are not only apparent to you but an integral part of your living. [This kind of thing is easy to understand and write about here at Steve Baer’s.]

I’ll do the very best | can to make a personal and honest Michael Rosenthal first hipped us to this remarkable set of U.N. documents which arose from a conference in Rome in 1961. If you’re deeply into solar or wind energy there’s lots of stuff here unfindable elsewhere. Japanese solar pillows-simple elegant water heaters up on the roofs, thoroughly described in Vol. 5. Traditional Dutch drainage windmills with suggested adaptations for other early-technology applications

opening_lines.text: Winning Time 2:32. Who Won Is Debated.
Aspen Meadows, New Mexico. Summer Solstice, 1969
Hugh Romney, who mustered this 1000 in this mountain
pasture, is announcing the start of the Bus Race and that a
At the starting line Hugh Romney mounts his bus.
Feverish last minute preparations aboard Further.
THE GREAT BUS RACE
Everyone will go one trial run around the course: % mile up
the meadow, around the flag, and back downhill to the starting
line. Majestically the line of buses staggers up the course. ‘‘I
couple named John and Mary are somewhere around and
be found because they have the bubonic plague and The survivors of the trial run line up. They’re immense,
must
require innoculation immediately. Everybody’s pretty
innoculated already; it’s the spaciest part of the afternoon.
The race was going to be one bus at a time against the clock,
but Ken Kesey and others are maintaining that’s a chickenshit
It’s got to be all at once. And it’s begun. Road Hog gets off first. Further is dragging.
The air is filled with buses and exhaust and din. They wallow
race.
_The buses are Kesey’s renowned Further, with a skeleton
crew of Farmsters aboard, and the Hog Farm’s fleet: Road
Hog,
tight
don't know about this,”” says Mike Hagen, ‘’Further ain't got
much brakes.’’ The Hospital Bus is stalled half-way up the
hill right where :t’s almost impossible to get around.
Reportedly it also ran over a pup tent (unoccupied).
these faded colorful wrecks filled with folly. People are
climbing off, others are climbing on. It’s a test of passengers
as much as drivers. The mood is pure: fatalistic optimism.
up the pasture like berserk pigs. As Further sways past the
grounded Hospital Bus Kesey disappears over the windshield
Hospital Bus, Kitchen Bus, etc. The Motherfuckers’ shouting orders to driver Bucavich. Over there in the bushes
little bus is around but not in the race.
poveane toward the first turn Kesey has an excellent view of the rear of the Hog Farm
eet.
the Foot Swami from India is watching it all go by.
Tied up at the flag, Romney
is visible on the track below.
